data.body:
## Problem

The normalized `result` event has been the focus of at least 5 prior fixes
(commits 79bf3c15, 41e10089, 1e32e0d4, 5063357b, 49a17082) yet agents — and
me, just now — keep mis-explaining it: "we saw `result`, so the take-loop
must have spuriously interpreted that as failure". That framing is wrong
under current code (49a17082 made `result` exactly = canonical turn boundary
sourced from `session.idle` for OpenCode, `result`/`turn.completed` for
Codex/Opus, never per-message `step_finish`).

Without a TAXONOMY.md entry that pins down what `result` means and what
behavior it does/does not authorize, every regression investigation will
re-derive the wrong story.

## Target state

`TAXONOMY.md` contains an entry titled **`result` event** (under Nouns,
or in a new "Normalized events" section) with all of the following, exact:

1. Definition: the canonical "this prompt-to-agent turn is over" signal
   emitted by transport adapters. NOT a generic "something happened" or
   "model produced output" signal.
2. Sources per transport:
   - OpenCode: `session.idle`, `session.status` with `status.type==="idle"`,
     and synthetic `step-finish` only when `reason==="error"`.
   - Codex: `result` JSON-RPC item-completed with `type==="result"`.
   - Claude/Opus: SDK `turn.completed`.
   - File path + line citation for each source mapping.
3. App behavior contract — what `result` DOES authorize:
   - Take-loop may evaluate whether to advance, follow-up, or stop.
   - Watchdog may reset its silence timer.
4. App behavior contract — what `result` does NOT authorize:
   - Does NOT mean the agent failed.
   - Does NOT mean the lease should be terminated.
   - Does NOT mean the beat state should change.
   - Does NOT mean a follow-up MUST be sent without lease/state checks.
5. Anti-patterns to grep for during review:
   - Any new code path that maps a per-message OpenCode `step-finish` to
     `result` (the 49a17082 regression class).
   - Any code that conditions destructive action (terminate lease, force
     state change) on a `result` event without further checks.

Also update the existing `Turn` entry (TAXONOMY.md:288) — its current
"one prompt in, one result out" wording is loose and reinforces the
misconception.

## Acceptance

- TAXONOMY.md `result` entry is present and matches the contract above
  verbatim where verbatim is specified.
- `Turn` entry is rewritten to reference the `result` entry rather than
  using "one result out" loosely.
- Citations point to the actual normalizer functions
  (`normalizeOpenCodeStepFinish`, `normalizeOpenCodeSessionIdle`,
  `translateSessionStatus`, etc) at their current line numbers.
- Lint/typecheck/test stay green.

## Why this matters

This is anti-regression infrastructure. The fix in 49a17082 is correct,
but the next time the symptom recurs, the explanation drifts again. A
normative TAXONOMY entry is the agent-readable contract that prevents
the next round of "obviously fucked all the possible things".
data.title: Document 'result' event in TAXONOMY.md with exact semantics and exact app-behavior contract
